Name: 21_ripgrep_rust_is_crate
Representative_Docs: ['add support for additional text encodings add support for additional text encodings this includes but is not limited to utf 16 latin 1 gbk euc jp and shift jis courtesy of the crate specifically this feature enables ripgrep to search files that are encoded in an encoding other than utf 8 the list of available encodings is tied directly to what the crate supports which is in turn tied to the encoding standard the full list of available encodings can be found here this pull request also introduces the notion that text encodings can be automatically detected on a best effort basis currently the only support for this is checking for a utf 16 bom in all other cases a text encoding of the default implies a utf 8 or ascii compatible source encoding when a text encoding is otherwise specified it is unconditionally used for all files searched since ripgrep s regex engine is fundamentally built on top of utf 8 this feature works by transcoding the files to be searched from their source encoding to utf 8 this transcoding only happens when 1 is specified and a non utf 8 encoding is detected 2 a specific encoding is given by end users including utf 8 when transcoding occurs errors are handled by automatically inserting the unicode replacement character in all other cases the source text is searched directly which implies an assumption that it is at least ascii compatible but where utf 8 is most useful in this scenario encoding errors are not detected this design may not be optimal in all cases but it has some advantages 1 in the happy path utf 8 everywhere remains happy i have not been able to witness any performance regressions 2 in the non utf 8 path implementation complexity is kept relatively low the cost here is transcoding itself a potentially superior implementation might build decoding of any encoding into the regex engine itself in particular the fundamental problem with transcoding everything first is that literal optimizations are nearly negated future work should entail improving the user experience for example we might want to detect more text encodings a more elaborate ux experience might permit end users to specify multiple text encodings although this seems hard to pull off in an ergonomic way fixes 1 initial pr message this is in progress work on adding utf 16 support to ripgrep this works but it needs to be cleaned up a bit before it can be merged x add tests where the input is utf 16 x add a flag that permits controlling this behavior we could elect to punt on this if the right ux choice isn t obvious x when reading anything but utf 8 we need to disable the memory map searcher it s not practical to transcode a memory mapped file in a way that isn t isomorphic to just using the normal incremental interface this should be done optimistically by bailing out of the memory map strategy if there s a bom rather than pessimistically to avoid additional syscalls in the happy path x the api for needs to be tweaked so that its internal buffer can be reused without this the normal utf 8 happy path causes ripgrep to be slower than grep if you want to try this pr out which would be most welcome then because of the memory map issue mentioned above you ll need to pass the flag to ripgrep to a first approximation ripgrep is approximately 5x slower searching utf 16 when compared to utf 8 interestingly this applies both to searching a single 1gb subtitle file and searching the linux repo with all source files converted to utf 16 at first i thought this seemed bad but itself isn t much faster certainly within the same order of magnitude it seems another exciting thing i learned is that gnu grep doesn t support searching utf 16 at all for some reason i thought it did so this is an even better feature than i previously thought', 'switch from docopt to clap there were two important reasons for the switch 1 performance docopt does poorly when the argv becomes large which is a reasonable common use case for search tools e g use with xargs 2 better failure modes clap knows a lot more about how a particular argv might be invalid and can therefore provide much clearer error messages while both were important 1 made it urgent note that since clap requires at least rust 1 11 this will in turn increase the minimum rust version supported by ripgrep from rust 1 9 to rust 1 11 it is therefore a breaking change so the soonest release of ripgrep with clap will have to be 0 3 there is also at least one subtle breaking change in real usage previous to this commit this used to work rg e foo where this would cause ripgrep to search for the string clap currently has problems supporting this use case see but it can be worked around by using this instead rg e foo or even rg foo and this still works rg foo this commit also adds bash fish and powershell completion files to the release fixes a bug that prevented ripgrep from working on file paths containing invalid utf 8 and shows short descriptions in the output of but longer descriptions in the output of fixes 136 189 210 230', 'various rollup move off of clap to lexopt cli replace clap with lexopt and supporting code ripgrep began it s life with docopt for argument parsing then it moved to clap and stayed there for a number of years clap has served ripgrep well and it probably could continue to serve ripgrep well but i ended up deciding to move off of it why the first time i had the thought of moving off of clap was during the 2 3 4 transition i thought the 3 x and 4 x releases were great but for me it ended up moving a little too quickly since the release of 4 x was telegraphed around when 3 x came out i decided to just hold off and wait to migrate to 4 x instead of doing a 3 x migration followed shortly by another 4 x migration of course i just never ended up doing the migration at all i never got around to it and there just wasn t a compelling reason for me to upgrade while i never investigated it i saw an upgrade as a non trivial amount of work in part because i didn t encapsulate the usage of clap enough the above is just what got me started thinking about it it wasn t enough to get me to move off of it on its own what ended up pushing me over the edge was a combination of factors as mentioned above i didn t want to run on the migration treadmill this has proven to not be much of an issue but at the time of the 2 3 4 releases i didn t know how long clap 4 x would be out before a 5 x would come out the release of lexopt 1 caught my eye imo that crate demonstrates exactly how something new can arrive on the scene and just thoroughly solve a problem minimalistically it has the docs the reasoning the simple api the tests and good judgment it gets all the weird corner cases right that clap also gets right and is part of why i was originally attracted to clap i have an overall desire to reduce the size of my dependency tree in part because a smaller dependency tree tends to correlate with better compile times but also in part because it reduces my reliance and trust on others it lets me be the master of ripgrep s destiny by reducing the amount of behavior that is the result of someone else s decision whether good or bad i perceived that clap solves a more general problem than what i actually need solved despite the vast number of flags that ripgrep has its requirements are actually pretty simple we just need simple switches and flags that support one value no multi value flags no sub commands and probably a lot of other functionality that clap has that makes it so flexible for so many different use cases i m being hand wavy on the last point with all that said perhaps most importantly the future of ripgrep possibly demands a more flexible cli argument parser in today s world i would really like for example flags like and to be able to accumulate their repeated values into a single sequence while respecting the order they appear on the cli for example prior to this migration would not return results in in this repository because the always took priority even though appeared after it but with this migration now correctly overrides we would like to do similar things for and and potentially even now introduce a flag instead of requiring users to use to negate a glob which i had done originally to work around this problem and some day i d like to add some kind of boolean matching to ripgrep perhaps similar to how does it although i haven t thought too carefully on a design yet in order to do that i perceive it would be difficult to implement correctly in clap i believe that this last point is possible to implement correctly in clap 2 x although it is awkward to do so i have not looked closely enough at the clap 4 x api to know whether it s still possible there in any case these were enough reasons to move off of clap and own more of the argument parsing process myself this did require a few things i had to write my own logic for how arguments are combined into one single state object of course i wanted this this was part of the upside but it s still code i didn t have to write for clap i had to write my own shell completion generator i had to write my own output generator i also had to write my own man page generator well i had to do this with clap 2 x too although my understanding is that clap 4 x supports this with that said without having tried it my guess is that i probably wouldn t have liked the output it generated because i ultimately had to write most of the roff by hand myself to get the man page i wanted this also had the benefit of dropping the build dependency on asciidoc asciidoctor while this is definitely a fair bit of extra work it overall only cost me a couple days imo that s a good trade off given that this code is unlikely to change again in any substantial way and it should also allow for more flexible semantics going forward fixes 884 fixes 1648 fixes 1701 fixes 1814 fixes 1966 1']

Name: 40_memory_batch_harvest_ram
Representative_Docs: ['growable tasks transition the three main tasks in the batch hacking system to using create new helper apis for use by both sow and harvest scripts to calculate phase ordering and spawn batches of multiple script types we significantly reworked how sow works changing from two allocations one for weaken threads one for grow threads to one allocation increased the chunk size to a balanced set of grow weaken threads 12 1 also made some other miscellaneous improvements to the batch hacking system to facilitate usage of the growable allocations like adding launch failure tracking and exponential backoff for the task selector task launching wrap the done port sending in an callback to avoid delaying exit of weaken script implementing harvest script releasing chunks as hacking level rises and max overlap needed reduces corrected the implementation of to adjust the so grow and release don t thrash the allocator overall a comprehensive improvement to the batch hacking system from experimental use in early bitnode 5 it appears to work substantially better the main remaining issue is that we don t cancel or shrink harvest jobs when much more valuable servers become available some sort of periodic rebalancing of tasks is probably necessary', 'fix sow allocation fix the way that sow tasks allocate memory previously sow tasks used a very simplistic allocation and helper script launching strategy it would allocate one chunk of memory for the grow scripts and one chunk for the weaken scripts this minimizes the chunk size and means that we can fit sow helper scripts into more fragmented ram however it also means that one of these two allocations could fail if the other is too large or worse they could be out of balance if one allocation gets shrunk and the other doesn t in addition because the two batches of scripts were launched without any delays the grow script would finish significantly before the weaken scripts looking a bit strange on the monitor depending on how long the gap is to fix this we ve changed the sow script to calculate batches similar to how harvest works this makes the minimum allocation chunk a balanced group of threads and the timing logic is reused from the harvest batches so the timing of batch endings is precise this opens the door to a minor optimization in how sow evolves since each batch is potentially smaller we don t get as much exponential grow bonus from larger thread counts so instead we stagger batch spawns so they end serially and each grow batch gets to multiply the effect of the previous ones it remains to be seen if this will actually end up speeding up the growing process but it seems like it s better than using smaller batches that spawn as quickly as possible and then end in a somewhat unpredictable order', 'batch change harvest to use growable allocations change harvest to match till and sow task scripts in using the new growable allocations this should cause all existing tasks to be prioritized for growth when new memory becomes available rather than the task selector greedily assigning new memory to new tasks there is one complication though because harvest creates a pipeline of batches that it continuously spawns as the old ones end adding new memory is not sufficient to get the harvest script to make use of it we need to explicitly extend the length of the pipeline and spawn batches until we ve filled the new allocation size with batches then we should return to the process of waiting for batches to end before spawning a replacement batch']

Name: 44_types_files_type_file
Representative_Docs: ['add extension to list of ruby file types hello this pr adds the extension to the ruby type it s a pretty common file extension in rails apps in my experience the is often pretty empty and only sets some stuff up while most of the code lives in various files', 'implement mime type support on unix systems please provide as much information as possible about what your pr aims to do prs with no description will most likely be closed until more information is provided if you re planing on changing fundamental behaviour or add big new features please open a github issue first before starting to work on it if it s not something big and you still want to contact us about it feel free to do so problem description describe the bug that you fixed feature request that you implemented or link to an existing issue describing it this pr addresses issue 491 regarding the inability to open imhex project files directly from various unix desktop environments nb this pr s scope is limited to unix systems as i have no access to macos systems and could not find a way to automatically create file associations on windows implementation description explain what you did to correct the problem in order for project files to be recognised as such we need to create a new mime type according to rfc 6838 section 3 2 additional application mime types should be defined with a vendor prefix nb this is preferred over the deprecated prefix although rfc 6838 states that the vendor should be a well known producer this pr proposes a new mime type for imhex project files and associates it with the file format it also implements small changes in the arch linux and rpm builds in order to correctly ship the mime xml file during installation additional things anything else you would like to say the implemented change has been manually tested in the following unix systems and graphical environments arch linux with gnome 47 2 arch linux with xfce4 fedora 41 workstation edition with gnome 47 0 fedora 41 plasma with kde plasma 24 08 2 ubuntu 22 04 5 with gnome 42 9 which tests the functionality of the following build types arch linux s packages debian packages rpm packages for arch linux and rpm packages this has been tested to ensure that post installation hooks such as regeneration of the mime database is correctly executed which it is no further changes than those implemented in the pr are needed', 'add and to type unless i m misunderstanding the purpose history of the type the lack of seems like a pretty big miss here since it is by far the most common extension for config files on unix systems see just about anything in for example is less common but also used a lot and so on other musings are you happy with the structure formatting of the type list some of the lines exceed 80 cols for example and then some of the types are broken into multiple lines even though there are only like three entries is there any formatting work i can do on this for you would it be easier to read if each pattern was on its own line or is that too much vertical space should i add an e g given that has zsh profile files should i also add c for on the subject of shell types it s kind of weird gets its own type which makes sense to me since it s out doing its own thing but having its own type when it s a more or less bourne ksh descended shell just like bash is a little strange especially when you consider that bizarro incompatible shells like tcsh are included with bash should i add postgresql for should i add a systemd has loads of different extensions c should i add a or similar for c i m thinking belongs with might consider for too this is common in the php world if we re considering lock files i suppose we should probably also add the ones for composer yarn c let me know if i m getting all over engineer y']

Name: 56_editor_pattern_the_text
Representative_Docs: ['fix evaluate doesn t work with undo the pattern editor was resetting the flag that marked text changes on every frame but resolving a text change may take more than one frame so instead i created a function that allows the resetting of the text changed boolean from view pattern editor currently when a pattern is evaluated the pattern editor will lose focus regardless of how evaluation was triggered this is specially annoying when using f5 or evaluate now whenever a pattern is evaluated focus is given to the pattern editor when evaluation ends jumptoline didn t work for empty lines', 'fix fixed save projects not being done after the first one currently setting the time interval to save the project has no effect other than creating one initial save if the input file is unsaved when a file is created and not saved it remains in a dirty state which prevented setting the state of the autosaved project to needing to be saved fixed by decoupling the state of the provider from the state of the autosave when a provider is detected as being dirty it always makes the autosave as being needed once the time interval has elapsed feat implemented the menus on the main menu bar that will be available when the text editor has focus it allows you to load and save patterns using open and save and will tack changes if files on disk are modified externally it also only opens the file chooser the first time you save a pattern file and subsequent changes save to the same file if you want to save into another file and have the new file be tracked you can use save as finally export doesn t track the file on disk at all this feature uses the same changes tracker class used elsewhere in imhex fix changed the defaults of various shortcuts that were using alt a key to avoid possible problems with some keyboards shouldn t affect end users as their shortcuts are loaded from internal file but those who complain about the alt key misbehaving will be asked to reset the keys to the new defaults in addition all globally accessible shortcuts were added the allow while typing flag so that they can be used in any field that accepts text new menu entries were added for debugging to make the pattern editor and the hex editor menus more like each other finally the call to registermainmenuentries when initializing views was moved to occur after the call to registerviews so that menus are not repeated when set for different views', 'fix find next and find previous not working on console with only one menu entry and having set it to use the text editor then the console losses that functionality since both the console and the text editor are on the same view it follows that they must share the menu entries in some cases it is necessary to determine which of the two editors needs to be processed inside the menu entry itself also an error that caused the creation of invalid coordinates when console is still empty was fixed by setting coordinates to 0 0 for empty files another problem was that left clicking on the console didn t select the word under the cursor but it does when left clicking on the text editor now it works in both the menu entries on the console were removed completely and swapped with a call to obtain the default edit menu for the context popup also the console in write only so some of the entries like or must be greyed out this pr is only to fix the bugs that exist currently more work is still needed for 1 removing the remaining shortcuts and creating new menu entries for them and 2 adding support for and in the pattern editor with path tracking i tested all combinations of menus and shorcuts with console and pattern editor and there were no problems i could detect']

sink this behaviour allows replacement logic to work correctly when we try to replace n with something else see for more details however this also means that the option is not respected when grouping matches together because max count is only enforced in a sink and if we have multiple matches on adjacent lines they will be passed to the sink together potentially going over the limit this pr fixes that by also calculating the number of matches in the searcher itself and breaking early in case max count is reached during the search this way we make sure we don t overcount the matches', 'search add support for counting individual matches fixes 566 as discussed in 566 this pr adds a flag that will count the individual matches instead of the matching lines this does essentially the same thing will always happen behind a flag i hope this won t have any performance implications i initially considered using the existing variable in and to keep track of individual matches if were passed in and used to keep track of individual match count and will override each other', 'probability distribution bug in regex based random string generation thank you so much for this project issue summary i ve discovered a probability distribution bug in pocketbase s regex based random string generation system that affects the randomness and uniqueness of generated identifiers problem description when using character classes in regex patterns for id generation the current implementation creates a severe bias toward smaller character ranges this is particularly problematic when using url safe base64 patterns like where single characters and appear far more frequently than they should the issue also affects the default pattern creating uneven distribution between letters and numbers examples of the problem here are some examples of generated strings using showing the excessive occurrence of and notice the strings are heavily biased toward and characters creating poor randomness distribution after this fix the generated strings look like this cause the current implementation in processes character classes incorrectly splitting into ranges for the pattern the regex compiler creates these range pairs range characters count current probability expected probability a b c z 26 20 40 6 a b c z 26 20 40 6 0 1 2 9 10 20 15 6 1 20 1 6 1 20 1 6 the problem current approach randomly selects a range first each gets 20 then picks a character from that range better approach each individual character should have equal probability 1 56 for 64 total characters result single characters get 12 8x higher probability than they should additional context the input pairs array for the base64 pattern becomes even when writing the regex explicitly as the compiler still optimizes it to ranges resulting in the same biased behavior proposed solution i ve developed a corrected implementation that uses weighted random selection to ensure uniform character distribution this assures a correct probability distribution each character gets equal probability performance benchmarks i conducted tests and benchmarks to validate both correctness and performance improvements benchmark results Name: 64_video_mobject_animations_vmobject
Representative_Docs: ['video work recent changes made during video work including refactors on matrix mobjects smooth transitions between fixed in frame and non fixed in frame states allow cameraframe reorient to take in all values relevant to its position simplifying mobject updater tracking fix computation of normals for surface allow vgroup group and lagged start to accept iterable and generators as arguments add mobject always and mobject f always syntax analogous to mobject animate for easier updater use reduce performance issues involved with adding many submobjects', 'video work various changes and bug fixes based on video work', 'new vmobjects and animations many little fixes and cleanups to manim mainly focussed on these two structural changes 1 the way vmobjects store their points as before a vmobject consists of a sequence of cubic bezier curves and the control points of these curves are held in the vmobject points array however previously disconnected components were treated as submobjects with a special is subpath flag now that notion of a subpath doesn t exist and it s all handled as part of the vmobject points array the main importance here is that it makes the logic of interpolating between arbitrary vmobjects simpler where bugs like this one shouldn t arise 2 animations now have a begin finish and clean up from scene methods the main goal here is to disambiguate the initialization of an animation with the moment that it is meant to actually begin this allowed for much simpler implementations of animationgroup and succession which are less prone to bugs like this one as an added bonus animations now play much more seamlessly with mobject updaters for example if you write then apply some animation to this square e g drawborderthenfill square that animation won t interrupt the constant right shift note no effort was made to make animation play nicely with continualanimation since the intention is to soon deprecate those note while some attempts were made to keep scenes from in old projects compatible with these changes it is very likely that some changes here will break the functionality of old scenes additionally there were the several changes to naming conventions arrange submobjects arrange print submobject family print family sort submobject sort shuffle submobjects shuffle dashed segment length dash length update mobject interpolate mobject for animation but not for continualanimation likewise for update submobject interpolate submobject lag ratio either in the context of laggedstart or in how an animation paces its submobject movements now means something quantitatively different but qualitatively similar hopefully it s now easier to reason about basically it represents some interpolation between multiple actions happening simultaneously lag ratio 0 and in succession lag ratio 1']

Name: 67_column_tables_table_header
Representative_Docs: ['fix group view column resize with hidden row numbers summary handle column width mapping using visible columns update detail tables to apply widths to visible columns after resize testing fails selector component specs', 'sync child supertables with group header summary propagate resize filter and sort events from the group header table update child tables when header changes occur expose helper methods on supertable clean up lint issues testing fails selector tests', 'feat table add headerrepeat option and fix dynamic layout overview improve page breaking logic so that when there are many pages table rows no longer overlap with the bottom margin or subsequent elements add a option to dynamic tables so that header rows can be repeated on each generated page when a table spans multiple pages related issues closes 706 table page break creates unnecessary extra page closes 629 add repeathead option for table with dynamic data background previously when a dynamic table was placed near the bottom of a page and spanned to the next page along with additional content the layout logic could let table rows intrude into the bottom margin push following content inefficiently sometimes resulting in an unnecessary extra blank page as described in 706 in addition there was no way to repeat the table header on every page for long tables which made multi page tables harder to read requested in 629 this pr introduces a long page page splitting layout flow that considers all pages schemas at once a option and header aware height calculation so headers can be repeated cleanly when tables are split across pages changes reworked flatten all template pages schemas into a single long page with preserving vertical order but offsetting across pages build a tree for that long page then call once to split it into multiple physical pages reconstruct a new via grouping split segments of the same into unified schemas per page with to mark which body rows for tables belong to the current page to indicate if the table is continued from a previous page enhanced track to avoid moving elements back to earlier pages when an element does not fit the remaining space move it forward page by page handling the case where an element s own height is larger than a single page s content height ensures elements including table parts and following text don t overlap and are compactly distributed across pages tests in adjusted existing multi page expectations to reflect the new more precise splitting behavior added test to verify a table starting at the bottom of page 1 and text on page 2 are laid out across two pages without generating an extra blank page the table s continuation and text appear together on the second page in the correct order types and schema behavior extended with an optional in preserve alternating background logic when is odd compute so that if headers are never shown otherwise show the header when the table is not split or the table is split but i e explicitly requested dynamic heights for tables now builds if is true heights of all rows head body if is false uses to align body rows without a rendered head computes using when is true enables header repetition only when is true is a blank pdf and simulates row by row placement across pages tracks the current page index current y position and the number of rows placed on the current page for each body row if it is the first row on a new page after the initial one adds to that row s effective height so the header s vertical space is accounted for uses a small safety margin to avoid off by a fraction overlap at page boundaries when a row including header row height doesn t fit if we are at the top of a page and the row plus header is still too tall place it on that page anyway otherwise move to the next page and retry returns the adjusted heights array which guarantees that headers are counted where they will be rendered rows do not overlap the bottom margin or spill inefficiently into extra blank pages prop panel and defaults added boolean field next to splitting the span into 12 12 set by default to keep existing behavior unchanged unless explicitly enabled schema validation extended in to include so that the new i18n key is validated i18n added translations for in all supported languages en zh ja ko ar th it pl de es fr so the prop panel label appears correctly in the designer ui impact user facing behavior multi page dynamic tables no longer overlap the bottom margin or leave unnecessary blank pages when combined with subsequent content can now repeat headers on each page when is enabled improving readability on long tables the default is so existing templates keep their visual behavior unless the new option is explicitly turned on compatibility is extended in a backward compatible way optional field with a default header repetition is only enabled for blank layouts behavior with custom pdfs remains consistent with previous limitations snapshot before img width 669 height 306 alt before src before pdf after img width 669 height 306 alt after src after pdf before pdf sample sample template json sample data json']

localsend directory', 'feat add clipboard sharing functionality this pr adds comprehensive clipboard sharing support to localsend allowing users to share clipboard content text images html urls and file paths between devices on the same network features added clipboard data support for text images html urls and file paths rust backend http endpoints for clipboard send receive operations flutter ui complete clipboard sharing interface with device selection state management integration with existing localsend architecture internationalization full translation support for all ui elements clipboard tab app assets i18n en json added translations the implementation follows localsend s existing patterns and architecture ensuring seamless integration with the current codebase', 'feature add live photo support for cross platform transfer feature add live photo support for cross platform transfer overview this pr implements live photo support for localsend enabling the preservation of apple s live photos when transferring files to ios devices live photos capture not just a moment but the moments just before and after bringing memories to life with movement and sound making them far more vivid and emotionally engaging than static images why live photos matter live photos represent a revolutionary way to capture and preserve memories unlike traditional static photos live photos capture living moments record 1 5 seconds before and after the shutter press preserve emotional context include ambient sounds and subtle movements enhance memory recall studies show that motion and sound significantly improve memory retention future proof memories maintain the full richness of captured moments for future generations by supporting live photo transfer localsend ensures that these precious dynamic memories are preserved when sharing between devices rather than being reduced to static images summary live photo reception receive and save live photos on ios devices intelligent file pairing automatic matching of image and video components based on filename smart caching system temporary storage and pairing of live photo components with timeout management user interface integration toggle option for saving as live photo in receive settings only shown on supported devices device capability detection automatic detection of live photo support with graceful ui adaptation seamless integration works with existing localsend transfer protocols key changes core architecture live photo provider device capability detection caching system intelligent file pairing with timeout management native bridge ios native method calls ios implementation live photo handler complete ios photos framework integration metadata processing proper exif and quicktime metadata handling video processing h 264 encoding with original bitrate preservation error handling comprehensive error logging and recovery file pairing logic the system uses filename based matching to identify live photo pairs 1 filename extraction remove file extensions to get base filename 2 component matching match image files heic jpg with video files mov mp4 that share the same base name 3 automatic pairing when both components are received automatically combine them into a live photo 4 timeout protection if pairing fails within 5 minutes files are saved separately to prevent data loss example combined into live photo ui ux enhancements smart ui adaptation save as live photo toggle only appears on devices that support live photo functionality receive options new save as live photo toggle in receive settings ios only settings integration persistent user preferences internationalization support for english and chinese languages graceful degradation on unsupported devices the live photo option is completely hidden from the ui how to test prerequisites ios device with live photos capability ios 12 any device capable of sending files to localsend localsend installed on both devices test scenarios 1 live photo reception send paired image and video files with matching filenames from any device receive on ios device with save as live photo enabled verify live photo is created and saved correctly in photos app 2 ui adaptation install on ios device verify save as live photo option appears in receive settings install on android other devices verify save as live photo option is completely hidden 3 settings persistence toggle save as live photo option on ios restart app and verify setting is preserved 4 fallback behavior send only image or only video file verify files are saved separately when pairing fails platform support platform sending receiving ui option visibility ios 12 not yet full support save as live photo shown android not supported not supported option completely hidden windows macos linux not supported not supported option completely hidden current limitation this initial implementation focuses on receiving live photos on ios devices sending live photos from ios and android support are planned for future releases demo materials screenshots 1 not support save as live photo 1 not support save as livephoto 2 ios support save as live photo 1 ios support live photo 3 receive ios receive option page ios receive result ios gallory demo gif ios live photo receive page how to extend supporting android platforms to add android live photo support developers need to 1 research android live photo formats different manufacturers use different formats samsung motion photos google motion photos etc 2 implement android native code add live photo saving capabilities in the android mainactivity kt 7 update device detection logic extend the capability detection to include android devices 8 add method channel handlers implement android specific method channels for live photo operations 9 test across devices validate functionality across different android manufacturers and versions the existing architecture with and is designed to be extensible and will automatically support android once the native implementation is added known limitations current constraints ios reception only currently only supports receiving live photos on ios devices manual file pairing requires sender to manually select both image and video components filename dependency relies on matching filenames for component pairing no live photo preview cannot preview live photos during transfer selection device compatibility fully supported ios 12 devices for reception partially supported any device can send component files unsupported android live photo creation ios live photo sending ui behavior live photo options are completely hidden on unsupported devices outstanding issues transfer progress accuracy when merging image and video files into live photos the process blocks the sending thread causing inaccurate estimated completion times displayed on both sender and receiver ui the progress indicators may show misleading time estimates during the live photo processing phase though they will not show completion until the actual live photo creation is finished future roadmap planned enhancements complete documentation comprehensive user guides and developer documentation for live photo functionality android live photo support implement receiving and saving live photos on android devices with support for various manufacturer formats ios live photo sending enable sending live photos from ios devices including proper export and component separation fix transfer progress accuracy resolve ui progress estimation issues caused by live photo processing blocking the sending thread attachments download ios localsend livephoto zip related issues closes 1052 add export support for ios livephotos closes 1580 transfer apple live photos to android with localsend addresses multiple user reports about live photo preservation note this is the first phase of live photo support focusing on ios reception capabilities the implementation follows localsend s architecture patterns and maintains backward compatibility while laying the foundation for comprehensive live photo support across all platforms']

Name: 70_event_loop_mediumeventloop_loopfinished
Representative_Docs: ['fast shutdown for private eventgroups in mediumeventloop tidy ups this pr refines the new private eventgroup flow by enabling a fast teardown path in when is true allowing package level access to the flag for event loops simplifying a monitoring helper to use default behaviour for normal long lived groups is unchanged motivation private groups are short lived and can be shutdown inside the eventloop itself so it does work to wait for the thread to finish in the thread it s shutting down we also tidy up a small utility handler to reduce overhead changes visibility package private rationale allow same package loops to consult the flag without accessor overhead thread safety the flag is configured prior to start and not mutated thereafter fast shutdown path in effect private groups stop aggressively and return immediately standard groups continue to use graceful shutdown await lightweight closeable now extends instead of rationale keep idempotent close semantics with less overhead no allocation of creation traces which is appropriate for a tiny decorator used on the hot path', 'ensure eventhandler loopstarted loopfinished do not terminate mediumeventloop expected behaviour loopstarted loopfinished not called if eventloop not started on event loop start loopstarted called for each handler if exception thrown handler removed and loopfinished called while loop running if handler is added loopstarted is called and if exception thrown handler removed and loopfinished called calling loopfinished any exception failure should be logged as warning handler behaviour should not cause eventloop to terminate the action handler should not remove handler on exception the action handler should remove handler on invalideventhandlerexception', 'allow short lived private eventgroups to self shutdown from within introduce a private group mode for s intended to be short lived and not shared when enabled handlers running on the group s own thread may legally trigger shutdown from inside the loop by default nothing changes standard groups remain long lived and still reject in loop shutdown attempts motivation some subsystems e g ephemeral discovery warm up probes conditional one shot tasks spin up an quickly determine it isn t needed and should tear it down immediately today groups are designed to be long lived attempts to close from within the loop throw forcing awkward out of band signalling this pr provides a first class explicit escape hatch for these short lived cases what changed add and setter relax in loop close guard only throw when not a private group improve exception context include and interface add to allow propagation of the mode override and propagate to if present add builder flag new fluent api apply the flag during build behaviour compatibility default behaviour unchanged is unless set in loop close attempts still throw private groups when is used a handler running on the group s own thread may call without tripping the guard api note adding a method to is a source binary breaking change for third party implementations chronicle maintained implementers are updated in this repo external implementers must add a matching method usage example rationale problem fix impact problem ephemeral groups needed to tear down from within the loop but the framework prevented it requiring extra signalling logic fix introduce an explicit private group mode and relax the in loop close assertion only for that mode provide builder plumbing and propagation so all constituent loops share the same policy impact cleaner deterministic shutdown for short lived non shared groups no change for standard long lived groups']

Name: 80_provider_oauth2_oauth_email
Representative_Docs: ['oauth 2 0 login with access token hi i m using nextjs and nextauth js with an oauth provider strava as document at nextauth js the library includes some callbacks for using an oauth provider to hook into the login process however it does not include a callback to get the code returned by the provider to request the access token this pull request implements a pocktebase oauth login with an access token sent by the user rather requesting the access token with a code by itself to have not a major change for the js library i ve used some convention the redirecturl in the js method client users authviaoauth2 must include the oauth2 token struct as query params as follows only the query param access token is a must have the host is not relevant for but must be valid address to be validated by the original implementation the other token details are used for later implementation to store the token in the user s profile the js is as follows not relevant then userdata console log pocketbase user data json stringify userdata undefined 2 return true catch err console log error requesting auth signin at pocketbase err return false feature request for better implementation implement an own api endpoint which takes the oauth2 token as the input for querying the user s detail implement the new endpoint in the js library unfortunately this pull request includes the other one as well i ve created this branch based on the oauth strava branch maybe i should create a branch based in the main branch', 'add linear oauth provider hi i ve added linear as an oauth provider based on previous oauth integrations testing locally i ve checked everything several times pr code pocketbase ui client integration linear dashboard client id client secret but i m still getting a failed to fetch oauth2 token error on with as details note i ve set oauth2 redirect success just before being automatically closed if it doesn t work for you also let me know how i can fix it', 'strava as oauth provider ui example hi my use case is to use strava as an oauth provider for authentication in general quite easy to implement a new oauth provider additionally i ve updated the admin ui no strava logo and adding an example ui auth to test the oauth implementation but the implementations includes one hack the strava oauth userinfo endpoint does not returny any email address the current sign up sign in implementations requires a valid email address the implementation creates a fake email address as follows stravaid strava com this means the oauth user can not natch to any existing user and any email sent to the user will not work additional feature request for later implementation general oauth provider implement a general oauth provider without any names urls and scopes the general oauth provider may be initiated multiple times let the user initiate the general oauth provider multiples times the identifier should be the name given in the setting by initiating the provider let the user decide how to specify the scopes see comma separated list of scopes let the user decide how to extract the user s information registering via oauth without any email address the current implementation for authentication registration signin is based on an unique identifier which is the email address as seen at the strava implementation some oauth providers do not return any email address by design this results in an error at the oauth flow registration fails because the email is used as the primary unique identifier at the signin process i would propose something as follows let the admin define a setting sign up sign in without email address the oauth user is allowed to sign up sign in without any email address let the admin define a setting oauth users without any email address must verify their email the oauth user signs up without any email address the user is created but marked as email not verified blank the sign in api returns user must verify their email address with an hash key to enter the email address later i would let the user sign in via the oauth provider and it s the user s ui s responsibility to add an email address an example may be keycloak which let s you to configure that the user needs to enter an email address']

Name: 99_zsh_completion_sourcing_zshrc
Representative_Docs: ['add zsh and bash completion for gh release sh this change adds zsh and bash completion for the script making it easier to use from the command line the completions include options for and releases pr created automatically by jules for task started by arran4', 'refactor completion function hey again i ve been collecting issues left over from my previous effort and i think i ve found as many as i can for now the biggest problem which was carried over from the original was that option completion was basically broken on zsh 5 1 the latest version available in ubuntu xenial but i also found some typos in option names missing actions for options that take arguments missing exclusion rules c in addition to fixing bugs i improved the handling slightly and i added completion for sequences of types when given and i re ordered the options to match the output like i mentioned wanting to do before i tested this on zsh 5 1 zsh 5 2 and zsh 5 3 and it seems ok really hard to test properly though so i m sure i ll find something again', 'improve zsh completion i d been meaning to update this and i think it s in a state now where it s much better than it was i propose that we give this a few days to sit before merging just to make sure i haven t messed anything up it is a decent number of lines changed it s been working for me so far in zsh 5 3 and 5 5 though if any zsh users watching the repo care to test that would be pretty cool full details i ve updated the function to use spec grouping with this makes it so much easier imo to read and maintain option exclusions the down side is that grouping is only available in zsh 5 4 released in 2017 on older versions of zsh i simply strip the groups out this makes option exclusivity pretty inaccurate on those older versions but i think it s worth it it s not like it breaks anything it just continues to show options that might not be useful with options you ve already supplied i mentioned previously that i wasn t sure whether to complete rarely used negation options like and i decided i would complete them but only if it seems like the user is specifically requesting them or if they ve enabled a style requesting that they always be completed so if you do kbd tab kbd you won t get the million options but you will if you do kbd tab kbd to be clear this only affects the mostly newer options that were added solely to negate the effects of other options stuff that s more generally useful like and is still completed unconditionally i ve improved the accuracy of option exclusivity for zsh 5 4 i ve improved several option optarg descriptions i ve improved completion of colour whens c they now have descriptions i ve improved completion of colour specs in particular the handling of the colons this was the biggest irritation for me with previous iterations of the function tbh i ve removed some unnecessary work arounds i ve generally replaced some uncommon undesirable constructs by more idiomatic ones quoting stuff tag names to support users with off c eta the ci script failed on some of the appveyor platforms because they had an older version of zsh than i expected hopefully fixed']
